explicit_text: Giovanni Andreozzi was my father's biological father who came from Falvaterra, Lazio, Italy to the United States in 1909. He settled in Lackawanna, New York, near the steel plant on the shores of Lake Erie. Giovanni married my grandmother Vincenza Limongiello in late 1916, and they had three children, including my father Filippo. Sadly, Giovanni died in November 1918 from the Spanish flu at Columbus Hospital in Buffalo, which was the Italian hospital at the time.

explicit_text: The Spanish flu pandemic tragically affected my family in 1918. My grandfather Giovanni Andreozzi died suddenly in November 1918 from the flu while being treated at Columbus Hospital in Buffalo, which was the Italian hospital. explicit_text: Filiberto Covelli was my maternal grandfather who moved back and forth between Italy and the United States starting in 1899. He initially went to Chicago, then worked in Kenosha, Wisconsin, before moving to Lackawanna, New York for better-paying jobs. Filiberto married Rosina Ruffalo, and after her sudden death in 1925, he remarried a woman named Gelsomina. He finally brought his family to the United States in December 1927.
